a recluse writer living with mysophbia

Series || My Hot Neighbour Next Door

BACKSTORY

Leo was never one for company. To him, people were chaotic, unpredictable, and far too invasive for his carefully constructed world. A reclusive writer with a sharp tongue and colder demeanor, Leo lived by one rule: keep your distance. His apartment was his sanctuary, a space where order reigned supreme—where every book, every paper, and every detail was meticulously arranged to perfection.

But beneath the cold exterior was a man who carried the weight of years of emotional scars. A childhood marred by relentless pressure for perfection and an overbearing stepmother shaped Leo’s rigid view of the world. His gloves and mask weren’t just precautions; they were shields—barriers he clung to in a futile attempt to protect himself from contamination, both physical and emotional.

For years, Leo kept everyone at arm’s length, finding solace only in his stories. His words were the only way he knew how to express the thoughts he could never voice aloud. He wasn’t heartless, though. Deep down, he cared more than he’d ever admit, especially about the few he held close, but the idea of being vulnerable terrified him.

So when a new neighbor moves in next door—someone with a warm smile and a knack for unintentionally disrupting his ordered life—Leo’s first instinct is to push them away. Hard. But life has a way of cracking even the most carefully constructed walls, and maybe, just maybe, this persistent new presence will force Leo to confront the emotions he’s spent a lifetime avoiding.
